Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family, COMT subfamily
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. COMT subfamily. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=(+)-6a-hydroxymaackiain + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = (+)-pisatin + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:35471, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:43129, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:67347; EC=2.1.1.270; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:17707445, ECO:0000269|PubMed:1929414}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=a 4'-hydroxyisoflavone + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = a 4'-methoxyisoflavone + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:31739, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:63328, ChEBI:CHEBI:133959; EC=2.1.1.46; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:17707445, ECO:0000269|PubMed:1929414};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Methyltransferase involved in the phytoalexin pisatin biosynthesis. Has both 3- and 4'-O-methyltransferase activities. Can use (+)-6a-hydroxymaackiain, 2,7,4'-trihydroxyisoflavanone and with much less activity (+)-medicarpin as substrates, but not (-)-6a-hydroxymaackiain, daidzein, formononetin or isoliquiritigenin. May be involved in formononetin biosynthesis. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:17067644, ECO:0000269|PubMed:17707445, ECO:0000269|PubMed:1929414, ECO:0000269|PubMed:9349277}.

Can use (+)-6a-hydroxymaackiain, (+)-maackiain and with a lower activity (+)-medicarpin and 2,7,4'-trihydroxyisoflavanone as substrates, but not (-)-6a-hydroxymaackiain, daidzein, formononetin or isoliquiritigenin. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:17067644, ECO:0000269|PubMed:17707445, ECO:0000269|PubMed:9349277}.
